question_text: An astronaut has a mass of 80 kg. On Earth, the gravitational field strength is 10 N/kg. Calculate the astronaut's weight on Earth.
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula W = m × g, where m is mass in kg and g is gravitational field strength in N/kg. So 80 × 10 = 800 N. This shows how gravitational field strength determines how much an object weighs in a location.
